conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility criteria to ensure I can give you accurate guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your situation.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I have one child - Alex, who's 18 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To determine Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to understand their current situation.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school but aren't doing anything like that right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. I have a few more questions to fully assess the situation.

Is Alex currently working? If so, how many hours per week do they work?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

Is Alex currently receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit based on the rules.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: While you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, the rules are different for older children. For children aged 16 or over, you can only continue to receive Child Benefit if they are under 20 **and** they stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in approved education or training, they don't meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

However, if Alex's situation changes and they start approved education or training in the future, you could become eligible at that point. Additionally, if Alex were to register with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services after leaving education, Child Benefit could continue for up to 20 weeks.

If you have any questions about what counts as approved education or training, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for more information.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if they are under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 years old and is NOT currently in approved education or training, they do not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. The fact that Alex lives with you, is not working, and is not receiving benefits in their own right does not change this outcome - the key requirement is that children aged 16-19 must be in approved education or training to qualify for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and lives at home. While Alex is not working or claiming benefits, they are not in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, children aged 16 or over only qualify if they are under 20 and in approved education or training. Since Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
